artifact.true_job: A non-programmer-readable small-tool reliability experiment showing that Loop Anything moves a plausible receipt splitter first draft from simple-case success to reproducible rule reliability across named receipt rules and edge cases.

runtime.subagent_mechanism: Task tool with isolated generalPurpose subagents

facets.name: Rule correctness
facets.mission: Verify the final receipt splitter obeys the named money rules: item ownership, discount order, tax/tip allocation, cent reconciliation, and clear invalid-input rejection.
facets.distinctness_rationale: A tool can be reproducible and readable while still failing the actual money rules.

facets.name: Edge-case and reproducibility
facets.mission: Verify deterministic evidence, unchanged T01-T11 tests, rounding reconciliation, invalid-input coverage, and reproducible commands.
facets.distinctness_rationale: A tool can follow core rules in examples while still lacking reproducible proof or edge-case coverage.

facets.name: README evidence readability
facets.mission: Verify non-programmer readers can understand the before/after value from plain money rules and a pass/fail table without reading code.
facets.distinctness_rationale: A tool can be correct and reproducible while still failing as README evidence if readers need code knowledge to understand it.

evidence_items.description: Human-readable rules and deterministic T01-T11 test cases.
Baseline deterministic test result: 5/11 tests, raw score 25/55, normalized 45.5/100.
Loop final deterministic test result: 11/11 tests, raw score 55/55, normalized 100.0/100.
Deterministic local test runner used for both baseline and final.
Final reviewer outputs all include Verdict PASS, Score 120, non-empty Evidence checked, Must-fix issues none, and 120-level approval statements.

rounds.notes: Rule-based revision created from baseline to satisfy T01-T11 receipt rules.

token_estimate.note: Estimated from visible text only; not billing token usage.
